systemEffect: Loss of aircraft, loss of control, rotor burst, or catastrophic uncontained engine failure
scoringGuidance: Use when the credible propagated consequence can threaten aircraft survival.

systemEffect: Uncontained release, fire hazard, severe aircraft damage, or emergency diversion condition
scoringGuidance: Use for effects that can create hazardous aircraft-level conditions even if aircraft loss is not stated.

systemEffect: In-flight shutdown, engine shutdown, engine failure, major thrust loss, or forced operational response
scoringGuidance: Use when the engine can no longer provide intended propulsion or must be shut down.

systemEffect: Surge, stall, flameout, high vibration, loss of oil pressure, or immediate crew/maintenance action
scoringGuidance: Use when the engine remains installed but safe operation margins are seriously reduced.

systemEffect: Reduced thrust or performance, overtemperature margin loss, EGT margin loss, or turbine distress
scoringGuidance: Use when dispatch or continued operation may be limited by degraded engine performance.

systemEffect: Metallic particles, oil debris, downstream component damage, early shop visit, or component replacement
scoringGuidance: Use when the consequence is maintenance significant but not clearly an immediate operational hazard.

systemEffect: Local damage, abnormal noise, leakage without fire/shutdown wording, or inspection before continued use
scoringGuidance: Use when the effect appears local and controlled with limited propagation.

systemEffect: Observable degradation or monitored condition with low operational impact
scoringGuidance: Use for low-impact effects that do not require immediate removal or operational restriction.

systemEffect: Cosmetic or low-level wear with planned maintenance only
scoringGuidance: Use when evidence indicates little effect on system function.

systemEffect: No credible engine, aircraft, mission, or maintenance consequence
scoringGuidance: Use only when evidence explicitly supports negligible consequence.
